text: The transected proximal descending colon is prepared for end colostomy formation during a laparoscopic abdominoperineal resection for low rectal cancer. Below, the surgical specimen is visible, ready for extraction.

Using a near-infrared enabled laparoscope, the surgeon examines the colostomy perfusion with fluorescence angiography after systemic injection of indocyanine green (ICG). The colostomy appears dark, indicating poor perfusion, while a small segment of mesocolon fluoresces brightly. The proximal colon shows better fluorescence, suggesting a more suitable site for stoma formation to prevent postoperative complications.
Under white light, the surgeon examines the colostomy site, where the perfusion discrepancy observed earlier with near-infrared fluorescence angiography is not visible.
